invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
Successful USER and PASS authentication is required before sending LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE, or MLSD.
A data channel setup command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) should be issued prior to any command that opens a data connection (LIST, RETR, STOR).
If PASS fails (e.g. response 530), the client should restart the sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by an RNFR command.
QUIT can be sent at any time to terminate the control connection.
USER.username: MUST specify a username string
PASS.password: MUST specify a password string
TYPE.type-code: MUST be 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary
PORT.host-port: MUST be in format h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2
CWD.path: MUST specify the path to change to
RETR.filename: MUST specify the filename to download
STOR.filename: MUST specify the filename to upload
RNFR.pathname: MUST specify the source filename for renaming
RNTO.pathname: MUST specify the destination filename for renaming

RNTO must be preceded by an RNFR command.
QUIT can be sent at any time to terminate the control connection.

Some inferred states are weakly connected in the current model; add directed seeds for: DATA_TRANSFER

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

invariants_created: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
Successful USER and PASS authentication is required before sending LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE, or MLSD.
A data channel setup command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) should be issued prior to any command that opens a data connection (LIST, RETR, STOR).
If PASS fails (e.g. response 530), the client should restart the sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by an RNFR command.
QUIT can be sent at any time to terminate the control connection.
USER.username: MUST specify a username string
PASS.password: MUST specify a password string
TYPE.type-code: MUST be 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary
PORT.host-port: MUST be in format h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2
CWD.path: MUST specify the path to change to
RETR.filename: MUST specify the filename to download

RNTO.pathname: MUST specify the destination filename for renaming

status_changes.rationale: Trace evidence shows 331 (password required) in response to USER, which is the definition of pending authentication.
Trace evidence shows 230 (Logged in) in response to PASS.
Trace evidence shows 530 (Not logged in), which resets the authentication attempt.
Trace evidence shows 150 (File status okay; about to open data connection) for LIST when a channel was prepared.
Trace evidence suggests some servers may proceed to data transfer without explicit PORT/PASV if defaults are used.
Trace evidence shows 221 (Service closing control connection).
Trace evidence shows 221 (Service closing control connection) even before login.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: RFC 959 specifies the login sequence starts with USER and follows with PASS. The provided spec evidence confirms this.

status_changes.description: A data channel setup command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) should be issued prior to any command that opens a data connection (LIST, RETR, STOR).
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP requires establishing a data port/IP before transferring data. Spec evidence supports this requirement.

status_changes.description: If PASS fails (e.g. response 530), the client should restart the sequence with USER.
status_changes.rationale: A 530 error indicates authentication failure, and standard clients revert to the USER command to retry.

status_changes.description: QUIT can be sent at any time to terminate the control connection.
status_changes.rationale: QUIT is the standard way to close an FTP session from any state.

status_changes.description: CWD.path: MUST specify the path to change to
status_changes.rationale: CWD requires a target directory argument.

status_changes.description: RETR.filename: MUST specify the filename to download
status_changes.rationale: RETR requires a source filename argument.

status_changes.description: STOR.filename: MUST specify the filename to upload
status_changes.rationale: STOR requires a destination filename argument.

status_changes.description: RNFR.pathname: MUST specify the source filename for renaming
status_changes.rationale: RNFR requires the path of the existing file.

status_changes.description: RNTO.pathname: MUST specify the destination filename for renaming
status_changes.rationale: RNTO requires the new path for the file.
